signal_name: Developers maintain custom memory/handoff files around coding agents
notes: Seed example. Validate with specific issue threads, Reddit posts, and user interviews before treating as confirmed demand.

signal_name: Developers building SQLite/vector DB/knowledge graph memory infrastructure on top of AI coding agents
notes: Rough UX: claude-brain 67k+ messages despite amnesia; builder kept using Claude through daily frustration. Tutorials: 5+ unprompted build writeups in 2026. Incumbent: no named product displaced. API: Hmem and Recall as MCP servers. Hacks: grov (SQLite proxy) A-MEM (ChromaDB) claude-brain private-journal-mcp Hmem Recall temporal-bridge — 7+ distinct independent builds. Vocab: compression amnesia / memory OS forming. Copycats: 7+ tools. Outside: Milvus and MindStudio blogs reaching beyond core devs. Gap: no monetization evidence; all tools are open-source or free.

signal_name: Users maintaining 15k-token global CLAUDE.md files as personal agent OS configuration
notes: Rough UX: managing 15k-token files and strict 250-line limits per file rather than switching tools. Tutorials: Complete Guide to CLAUDE.md (Medium); Designing CLAUDE.md correctly: The 2026 architecture; multiple architecture guides. Incumbent: replaces human onboarding docs and pair-programming hand-off. API: AGENTS.md auto-loading (#25882) and CLAUDE.md execution (#34763) requests — indirect. Hacks: 15k-token global CLAUDE.md; multi-file length-limited systems; git-based /handoff /sync /engineering; rule-porter. Vocab: CLAUDE.md architecture and 250-line compliance limit becoming shared conventions. Copycats: practices not yet products — rule-porter is the clearest tool. Outside: ccforpms.com reaching non-engineers; Complete Guide on Medium. Gap: no evidence on how often these files are maintained or updated; time cost uncaptured.

signal_name: Cursor native memory removed — users migrate to manual project.md workarounds rather than leaving
notes: Rough UX: feature broken for months then removed; users stayed and downgraded to v2.0.77. Tutorials: OpenSpec framework and project.md convention referenced but no dedicated tutorial captured. Incumbent: strong — native Cursor memory clearly replaced by manual project.md and project rules. API: none captured. Hacks: project rules conversion; project.md files; OpenSpec; version downgrade — four distinct workarounds. Vocab: none captured. Copycats: platform bug/removal event not attracting competing tools in captured evidence. Outside: none captured. Gap: small sample (4-5 direct reporters); Cursor-specific failure may not generalize; unknown whether workarounds are temporary or permanent.

notes: Rough UX: same CRITICAL instructions prepended to every single sub-agent call; 6+ duplicate issues mean many hit this and kept going. Tutorials: none captured. Incumbent: replaces human expert hand-holding; no named product. API: three independent AGENTS.md issues (#6235 #34235 #25882) plus directory-based agents (#39641) and CLAUDE.md execution (#34763) — strong integration demand. Hacks: CRITICAL-instruction prepending; wrapper scripts implied but not specifically captured — scored 1 not 2. Vocab: none captured. Copycats: no tools targeting sub-agent statefulness specifically. Outside: none captured. Gap: workaround is prompt-level and invisible — hard to observe or quantify; wrapper script behavior not confirmed in evidence.
